testcase.name: runAllTests
testcase.system-out:
Running: TC-ID001 HTTP/1.1 200 OK date: Sun, 22 Mar 2026 14:05:18 GMT server: uvicorn content-length: 4117 content-type: application/json
{ "success": true, "text": "--- Page 1 ---
1. Which of the following legislations does NOT govern the imposition of income tax in Singapore?
A. Insurance Act.
B. Income Tax Act.
C. Goods and Services Tax Act.
D. Economic Expansion Incentives Act.

2. Leslie is employed and physically present in Singapore for 185 days during the year preceding the Year of Assessment. Henceforth, his tax status is defined as:
A. Pioneer.
B. Tax-free.
C. Resident.
D. Non-resident.

3. Steve qualified for “Not Ordinarily Resident” tax status in Singapore from 2019 and he will enjoy tax concessions for a period of __________ years.
A. three
B. four
C. five
D. six

4. Shirley has a statutory income of S$40,000 and an assessable income of S$30,000. If she is entitled to a personal tax relief of S$5,000, what is her chargeable income?
A. S$10,000
B. S$25,000
C. S$35,000
D. S$40,000

5. Amanda is a Singapore registered nurse working abroad. As an administrative concession, she can choose to be assessed for tax either as a resident or non-resident.
The required overseas employment is for a period of at least __________ months during the year preceding the Year of Assessment.
A. 3
B. 6
C. 9
D. 12", "page_count": 1, "successful_pages": 1, "failed_pages": 0, "char_count": 1159, "engine": "deepseek", "pages": [ { "page_num": 1, "success": true, "text": "1. Which of the following legislations does NOT govern the imposition of income tax in Singapore?
A. Insurance Act.
B. Income Tax Act.
C. Goods and Services Tax Act.
D. Economic Expansion Incentives Act.

2. Leslie is employed and physically present in Singapore for 185 days during the year preceding the Year of Assessment. Henceforth, his tax status is defined as:
A. Pioneer.
B. Tax-free.
C. Resident.
D. Non-resident.

3. Steve qualified for “Not Ordinarily Resident” tax status in Singapore from 2019 and he will enjoy tax concessions for a period of __________ years.
A. three
B. four
C. five
D. six

4. Shirley has a statutory income of S$40,000 and an assessable income of S$30,000. If she is entitled to a personal tax relief of S$5,000, what is her chargeable income?
A. S$10,000
B. S$25,000
C. S$35,000
D. S$40,000

5. Amanda is a Singapore registered nurse working abroad. As an administrative concession, she can choose to be assessed for tax either as a resident or non-resident.
The required overseas employment is for a period of at least __________ months during the year preceding the Year of Assessment.
A. 3
B. 6
C. 9
D. 12", "images": [
], "content_blocks": [ { "type": "text", "content": "1. Which of the following legislations does NOT govern the imposition of income tax in Singapore?
A. Insurance Act.
B. Income Tax Act.
C. Goods and Services Tax Act.
D. Economic Expansion Incentives Act.", "image_index": null, "width": null, "height": null }, { "type": "text", "content": "2. Leslie is employed and physically present in Singapore for 185 days during the year preceding the Year of Assessment. Henceforth, his tax status is defined as:
A. Pioneer.
B. Tax-free.
C. Resident.
D. Non-resident.", "image_index": null, "width": null, "height": null }, { "type": "text", "content": "3. Steve qualified for “Not Ordinarily Resident” tax status in Singapore from 2019 and he will enjoy tax concessions for a period of __________ years.

{ "success": true, "text": "--- Page 1 ---
1. Which of the following legislations does NOT govern the imposition of income tax in Singapore?
A. Insurance Act.
B. Income Tax Act.
C. Goods and Services Tax Act.
D. Economic Expansion Incentives Act.

2. Leslie is employed and physically present in Singapore for 185 days during the year preceding the Year of Assessment. Henceforth, his tax status is defined as:
A. Pioneer.
B. Tax-free.
C. Resident.
D. Non-resident.

3. Steve qualified for “Not Ordinarily Resident” tax status in Singapore from 2019 and he will enjoy tax concessions for a period of __________ years.
A. three
B. four
C. five
D. six

4. Shirley has a statutory income of S$40,000 and an assessable income of S$30,000. If she is entitled to a personal tax relief of S$5,000, what is her chargeable income?
A. S$10,000
B. S$25,000
C. S$35,000
D. S$40,000

5. Amanda is a Singapore registered nurse working abroad. As an administrative concession, she can choose to be assessed for tax either as a resident or non-resident.
The required overseas employment is for a period of at least __________ months during the year preceding the Year of Assessment.
A. 3
B. 6
C. 9

B. Income Tax Act.
C. Goods and Services Tax Act.
D. Economic Expansion Incentives Act.

2. Leslie is employed and physically present in Singapore for 185 days during the year preceding the Year of Assessment. Henceforth, his tax status is defined as:
A. Pioneer.
B. Tax-free.
C. Resident.
D. Non-resident.

3. Steve qualified for “Not Ordinarily Resident” tax status in Singapore from 2019 and he will enjoy tax concessions for a period of __________ years.
A. three
B. four
C. five
D. six

4. Shirley has a statutory income of S$40,000 and an assessable income of S$30,000. If she is entitled to a personal tax relief of S$5,000, what is her chargeable income?
A. S$10,000
B. S$25,000
C. S$35,000
D. S$40,000

5. Amanda is a Singapore registered nurse working abroad. As an administrative concession, she can choose to be assessed for tax either as a resident or non-resident.
The required overseas employment is for a period of at least __________ months during the year preceding the Year of Assessment.
A. 3

{ "success": true, "text": "--- Page 1 ---
1. Which of the following legislations does NOT govern the imposition of income tax in Singapore?
A. Insurance Act.
B. Income Tax Act.
C. Goods and Services Tax Act.
D. Economic Expansion Incentives Act.

2. Leslie is employed and physically present in Singapore for 185 days during the year preceding the Year of Assessment. Henceforth, his tax status is defined as:
A. Pioneer.
B. Tax-free.
C. Resident.
D. Non-resident.

3. Steve qualified for “Not Ordinarily Resident” tax status in Singapore from 2019 and he will enjoy tax concessions for a period of __________ years.
A. three
B. four
C. five
D. six

4. Shirley has a statutory income of S$40,000 and an assessable income of S$30,000. If she is entitled to a personal tax relief of S$5,000, what is her chargeable income?
A. S$10,000
B. S$25,000
C. S$35,000
D. S$40,000

5. Amanda is a Singapore registered nurse working abroad. As an administrative concession, she can choose to be assessed for tax either as a resident or non-resident.
The required overseas employment is for a period of at least __________ months during the year preceding the Year of Assessment.
A. 3
B. 6
C. 9

2. Leslie is employed and physically present in Singapore for 185 days during the year preceding the Year of Assessment. Henceforth, his tax status is defined as:
A. Pioneer.
B. Tax-free.
C. Resident.
D. Non-resident.

3. Steve qualified for “Not Ordinarily Resident” tax status in Singapore from 2019 and he will enjoy tax concessions for a period of __________ years.
A. three
B. four
C. five
D. six

4. Shirley has a statutory income of S$40,000 and an assessable income of S$30,000. If she is entitled to a personal tax relief of S$5,000, what is her chargeable income?
A. S$10,000
B. S$25,000
C. S$35,000
D. S$40,000

testcase.system-err: ERROR StatusLogger Log4j2 could not find a logging implementation. Please add log4j-core to the classpath. Using SimpleLogger to log to the console...
